code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Group triples by subject to allow multiple entities
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Simple predicate → phrase templates
    # Helper to format numeric objects with commas and strip trailing .0
    def _fmt(val: str) -> str:
        try:
            num = float(val)
            if num.is_integer():
                return f"{int(num):,}"
            else:
                # keep one decimal if needed
                return f"{num:,}"
        except ValueError:
            return val.strip('"')

    templates = {
        # Airport‑specific predicates
        "cityServed": "serves {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "is {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is {obj} feet above sea level",
        # more natural phrasing with explicit units
        "runwayLength": "runway length is {obj} metres",
        "runwayName": "runway name is {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {obj}",
        "country": "is in {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "ICAO code is {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": "location identifier is {obj}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "IATA code is {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway length of {obj} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway length of {obj} metres",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "first runway surface is {obj}",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "first runway number is {obj}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "runway surface is {obj}",
        # Generic entity predicates
        "leader": "{obj} is a leader of {subject}",
        "capital": "{obj} is the capital of {subject}",
        "language": "{obj} is spoken in {subject}",
        "officialLanguage": "the official language of {subject} is {obj}",
        "currency": "the currency of {subject} is {obj}",
        "owner": "{subject} is owned by {obj}",
        "foundingYear": "{subject} was founded in {obj}",
        "founder": "{subject} was founded by {obj}",
        "postalCode": "{subject}'s postal code is {obj}",
        "areaCode": "{subject}'s area code is {obj}",
        "largestCity": "the largest city of {subject} is {obj}",
        "countySeat": "the county seat of {subject} is {obj}",
        "mayor": "the mayor of {subject} is {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "the leader party of {subject} is {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "the leader title of {subject} is {obj}",
        "chief": "the chief of {subject} is {obj}",
        "hubAirport": "the hub airport of {subject} is {obj}",
        "regionServed": "{subject} serves the region {obj}",
        "nativeName": "the native name of {subject} is {obj}",
        "jurisdiction": "{subject} has jurisdiction in {obj}",
        # Biological classification predicates
        "class": "the class of {subject} is {obj}",
        "division": "the division of {subject} is {obj}",
        "order": "the order of {subject} is {obj}",
        "species": "the species of {subject} is {obj}",
        "demonym": "the demonym for {subject} is {obj}",
    }

    sentences = []
    for subject, tlist in subject_map.items():
        parts = []
        for t in tlist:
            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                # Strip surrounding quotes from objects if present
                obj = t.object.strip('"')
                parts.append(tmpl.format(obj=obj))
            else:
                # Fallback generic phrase
                pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
                obj = t.object.strip('"')
                parts.append(f"{pred} {obj}")
        # Combine parts into a fluent sentence
        if parts:
            if len(parts) == 1:
                sentence = f"{subject} {parts[0]}."
            else:
                sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1] + "."
            sentences.append(sentence)

    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
